justifications.ip1: All three ACs have specific failing test scenarios: T1 asserts 'ACCEPTED pacs.002 when processing completes in 100ms' (AC1), T2 asserts 'REJECTED pacs.002 with SCHEME_SLA_EXCEEDED when processing stalls past 9,500ms' (AC2), and T3–T4 assert receipt/ack/elapsed fields on the log entry (AC3).
justifications.ip2: No out-of-scope items (fraud vendor API, outbound RTP, AML load testing, batch reconciliation) appear in any task, and C3's hard 9,500ms threshold is explicitly coded as a non-configurable constant `const SLA_THRESHOLD_MS = 9500` with the comment 'hard-coded, non-configurable'.
justifications.ip3: Every task names specific files (`tests/payments/rtp-sla-timer.test.js`, `src/payments/rtp-sla-timer.js`, `src/payments/rtp-message-handler.js`, `tests/payments/rtp-sla-timer.perf.test.js`) with concrete assertions such as `expect(ackCall.reason).to.equal('SCHEME_SLA_EXCEEDED')` and `expect(p99Value).to.be.lessThan(9000)`.
justifications.ip4: The 'Implementation Order (TDD)' section and Phase structure preserve test-first order (tests written in Task 1.1 before implementation in Task 1.2), but the plan omits an explicit 'run tests to confirm RED' step between Task 1.1 and Task 1.2 — the implementation section jumps straight from writing tests to implementation code without a separate RED-confirmation step.
justifications.ip5: NFR-1 has a dedicated T_NFR_1 performance test (`rtp-sla-timer.perf.test.js`) asserting `expect(p99Value).to.be.lessThan(9000)` at 200 messages/40 tps with real timers, and C3 is enforced as a hard-coded constant `SLA_THRESHOLD_MS = 9500` with an explicit note that no configurable override is permitted.

notes: Exceptionally strong plan with specific test assertions, correct file naming, hard-coded C3 constant, and a dedicated T_NFR_1 performance task; minor deduction is that no explicit 'run npm test → confirm RED' step appears between test authoring and implementation phases.
